issue: point_in_rect(px, py, x0, y0, x1, y1) wrongly reports points that lie exactly on the rectangle's right or top edge as being outside. The rectangle is meant to be inclusive of its boundary, so (10, 5) -- which sits on the right edge of the rectangle from (0, 0) to (10, 10) -- should count as inside, but the function returns False for it; likewise the top edge and the top-right corner. Strictly interior points and clearly-outside points are handled correctly.
